description: Team Context knowledge taxonomy — 7 cognitive categories based on 14 academic papers

categories.name: Domain Knowledge
categories.description: Technical and domain-specific knowledge organized by expertise area
categories.academicBasis: Walsh & Ungson (1991) organizational memory — structural retention bin
categories.subfolders: user-defined (e.g., frontend/, backend/, infra/, ai/, product/)

categories.name: Decisions & ADRs
categories.description: Architecture decisions, design choices, and their rationale
categories.academicBasis: Brown, Collins & Duguid (1989) situated cognition — knowledge bound to context

categories.name: People & Expertise
categories.description: Expertise maps — who has what knowledge, skills, and experience
categories.academicBasis: Wegner (1987) transactive memory systems; Ren et al. (2007) knowledge directories

categories.name: Procedures & Playbooks
categories.description: Step-by-step processes, runbooks, checklists, and onboarding guides
categories.academicBasis: Hutchins (1995) distributed cognition — procedural knowledge survives personnel changes

categories.name: Boundary Objects
categories.description: Specs, designs, agreements, and shared documents that bridge teams or roles
categories.academicBasis: Wenger (1998) communities of practice — boundary objects mediate knowledge across CoPs

categories.name: Tacit Knowledge
categories.description: Heuristics, lessons learned, patterns, and anti-patterns — tacit knowledge made explicit
categories.academicBasis: Nonaka & Takeuchi (1995) SECI model — externalization of tacit to explicit knowledge

categories.name: Glossary & Ontology
categories.description: Team-specific terms, acronyms, and naming conventions — shared vocabulary
categories.academicBasis: Collins & Quillian (1969) semantic networks — cognitive economy via shared hierarchy

designPrinciples.rule: No file duplication — use frontmatter tags for cross-cutting concerns
designPrinciples.source: Ranganathan (1933) faceted classification + Collins & Quillian (1969) cognitive economy

designPrinciples.rule: Folder names must match team mental model vocabulary
Structured metadata required on every document
designPrinciples.source: Lin et al. (2025) KBLaM — structured KB outperforms raw text for AI agents

designPrinciples.rule: Source and context fields mandatory — context-free knowledge is inert knowledge
Design organization + labeling + navigation + search systems together
Classify by actionability: reference vs actionable vs project-specific
